title: Tarphochlamys affinis
intro:
There is not enough information available about the plant Tarphochlamys affinis.

However, I was able to find some information on the genus Tarphochlamys. 

Tarphochlamys is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. 
Tarphochlamys plants are known for their potential health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. 
The bioactive constituents of Tarphochlamys plants include phenolic compounds and iridoids, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. 
The most common herbal preparations made from Tarphochlamys plants are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.

Please note that this information is based on the genus Tarphochlamys and not specifically on Tarphochlamys affinis, as I couldn't find any specific information on the latter.
taxonomy: Tarphochlamys affinis is classified within the family Ochnaceae. This classification places it within the order Malpighiales. The family Ochnaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the polypetalous family. The order Malpighiales is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes over 16,000 species. Tarphochlamys affinis is a unique species within this broader classification.
preparations: Tarphochlamys affinis, a plant species found in Australia, has been used in traditional herbal medicine. However, specific information on herbal preparations derived from this plant is limited. More research is needed to determine its potential medicinal properties and uses. In some cases, plants in the same family as Tarphochlamys affinis have been used to make medicinal teas and infusions. But it's essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional before using any herbal preparation.
